title: Simple 1x8 Open Shelf
summary: A small open shelf box with a flat metal bar across the front edge instead of a lip — just enough to keep things from sliding off.
description:
This is about as simple as a wall shelf gets: two 1x8 sides screwed into a bottom/back piece, with a strip of flat metal stock across the front standing in for a wood lip. The metal rail is a nice detail — thin enough to stay visually quiet, but effective at stopping jars or books from sliding forward.

Note for anyone comparing against the source listing: the shopping and cut lists here consistently call for 1x8 boards (7-1/4" actual width), even though some source titles for this plan say "1x6." Build from the 1x8 dimensions below — they're what the actual cut list requires.

Cut the metal bar with a jigsaw fitted with a metal-cutting blade, and don't expect it to add real structural strength — its job is just to keep small items from tipping off the front edge, not to carry load.

tools.note: Fitted with a metal-cutting blade to cut the flat bar stock.
Includes a metal bit for predrilling the bar stock.

materials.name: Pine, 1x8, 8 ft
materials.note: Sides and bottom/back — buy two boards for clean offcuts and to allow for knots.

materials.name: 3/4"-wide flat metal stock, 41" length
materials.note: Hardware-store bar stock, cut to length.

materials.name: 2" self-tapping wood screws or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws
Wall-mounting screws
materials.note: Choose for your wall type — 2-1/2" screws into studs through drywall is typical.

steps.title: Join the bottom and back
steps.body: Attach the two 1x8 boards together edge to edge with screws — either pocket hole screws or 2" self-tapping screws — placed about every 6–8" along the joint. This forms the L-shaped bottom/back of the shelf.
materials: 2" self-tapping wood screws or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws

steps.title: Attach the sides
steps.body: Glue and nail the two side pieces to the bottom/back assembly. These sides are the main structural piece holding everything else up, so make sure they're square and firmly fastened.

steps.title: Add the metal front rail
steps.body: Cut the flat metal stock to length with a jigsaw fitted with a metal-cutting blade — wear safety glasses, metal cutting throws sharp filings. Predrill it with a metal drill bit and attach it across the front edge; a single 1-1/4" screw at each end is plenty. It's meant to stop things from sliding off, not to bear weight, so don't over-engineer the fastening.

steps.title: Mount to the wall
steps.body: Locate studs with a stud finder and screw through the back of the shelf into them. On a typical stud-framed wall with 1/2" drywall, 2-1/2" screws are a solid choice.

images.alt: A simple open wall shelf with a thin metal bar across the front edge
